Strings.App.Title: ASD4G - Automatic Scaling Display 4 Gaming
Strings.App.Name: ASD4G
Strings.App.Subtitle: Automatic Scaling Display 4 Gaming
Strings.Operation.Title: Track scaling and monitoring in real time
Strings.Operation.Description: When one of your configured target programs starts, ASD4G will try to switch to the target resolution automatically. Once every matching program exits, the app restores the original display mode.
Strings.Operation.WorkflowDescription: The app keeps polling the exe paths that you add on the settings page. As soon as any configured program is running, ASD4G tries to switch to the target resolution. When all monitored programs exit, it restores the original display mode that was active before scaling started.
Strings.Settings.Title: Configure target apps, resolution, and language
Strings.Settings.Description: Use the sections below to configure auto start, the target program list, the target resolution, and the UI language. Presets are useful for a quick setup, while custom values allow fine-grained control.
Strings.Settings.AutoStartDescription: When enabled, the app starts automatically when the current Windows user signs in so it can keep listening for your selected programs.
Strings.Settings.TargetProgramsDescription: Use the plus button to select an exe. The list shows the icon and the full path. Once an item is selected, the minus button appears so you can remove it.
Strings.Settings.TargetPriorityHint: If multiple target apps match at the same time, ASD4G uses the resolution of the first app in the list.
Strings.Settings.ResolutionDescription: You can enter the target width and height directly, or use one of the preset options below. The app can only switch to modes that already exist in your current driver-supported display list.
Strings.Settings.GpuHint: It is recommended to disable stretched scaling in the NVIDIA or AMD control panel first, and to make sure the corresponding custom resolution already exists. Otherwise these non-standard resolutions may fail to apply.
Strings.Settings.DefaultResolutionScope: No target app is currently selected. The values below will be used as the default for newly added apps.
Strings.Settings.LanguageDescription: The app ships with Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese, and English. You can add more JSON translation files later in the Languages folder.
Strings.Status.WaitingForProcess: Monitoring is enabled and waiting for a target process.
Strings.Status.NoActiveProcess: No target process is currently running.
Strings.Status.TargetResolutionSupported: The target resolution is available in the current system mode list.
Strings.Status.TargetResolutionUnsupported: The target resolution is not available yet. Create it in your GPU driver first and confirm that Windows can use it.
Strings.Status.ManualScaleClosed: Scaling was manually turned off and the original resolution was restored.
Strings.Status.RestoredAfterExit: All target programs have exited and the original resolution has been restored.
Strings.Status.RestoredToOriginal: Monitoring stopped and the original resolution has been restored.
Strings.Status.ProcessDetected: A target program has been detected.
Strings.Status.CurrentModeUnavailable: The current display mode could not be read.
Strings.Status.ApplyFailed: Failed to switch to the target resolution. Make sure the mode is supported by both Windows and the GPU driver.
Strings.Status.RestoreFailed: Failed to restore the original resolution. Please check Windows display settings manually.
Strings.Status.TargetProgramExists: That program already exists in the list.
Strings.Status.AutoStartEnabled: Run at startup has been enabled.
Strings.Status.AutoStartDisabled: Run at startup has been disabled.
Strings.Status.AutoStartFailed: Failed to update the startup setting: {0}
